name: MongoDB Atlas Management from CLI
description: Manage MongoDB Atlas clusters, databases, and users from the command line using the Atlas CLI for automated cloud database operations.
trendingReason: Atlas is the dominant MongoDB hosting platform and CLI management enables infrastructure-as-code workflows
prerequisites: Atlas CLI installed (brew install mongodb-atlas-cli or equivalent)

steps.title: Authenticate with Atlas
steps.description: Log in to your Atlas account from the CLI.
steps.tip: This opens a browser for OAuth authentication. For CI/CD, use API keys: atlas config init --apiKey and --privateKey.

steps.title: List your Atlas projects
steps.description: View all projects (organizations) in your Atlas account.

steps.title: Create a free-tier cluster
steps.description: Deploy a new M0 (free tier) cluster in your project.
steps.command: atlas clusters create dev-cluster --provider AWS --region US_EAST_1 --tier M0
steps.tip: M0 is free forever with 512MB storage. Use M10+ for production workloads.

steps.title: Check cluster status
steps.description: View the status and connection details of your clusters.

steps.title: Create a database user
steps.description: Add a user with readWrite access for application connections.
steps.command: atlas dbusers create --username app_user --password secure_pass --role readWriteAnyDatabase --projectId <project-id>

steps.title: Get the connection string
steps.description: Retrieve the connection string to use in your application.
steps.tip: Use the 'mongodb+srv://' connection string format. It handles DNS-based service discovery and replica set routing automatically.

faq.question: Is the Atlas CLI free?
faq.answer: Yes, the Atlas CLI is free and open source. It works with all Atlas tiers including the free M0 tier. You only pay for the Atlas cluster resources you provision (M0 is always free).

faq.question: How do I use the Atlas CLI in CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: Create an API key in the Atlas web console under Organization > Access Manager > API Keys. Then configure the CLI with 'atlas config init' using the public and private API keys instead of interactive login.

faq.question: Can I manage Atlas with Terraform instead?
faq.answer: Yes, the MongoDB Atlas Terraform provider (mongodbatlas) supports full cluster, user, and network management as infrastructure-as-code. Use the Atlas CLI for quick operations and Terraform for reproducible infrastructure.
